basics.name: Anthony Armando Tirabassi
basics.label: University of Hawai'i at Mānoa Alumni
basics.summary: Aspiring Front-end Software Engineer and UI/UX Designer - B.A. Computer Science

education.institution: University of Massachusetts Boston, Boston, MA

highlights: Diagnosed hardware and software issues on Apple devices; assisted customers with setup, data transfer, and troubleshooting, and processed purchases and returns via Apple POS systems.

work.company: University of Hawai'i/Department of Hawaiian Home Lands
highlights: A project done in collaboration with the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands (DHHL) and the State of Hawai'i for the Tribal Broadband Connectivity Program (TBCP) to explore solutions to bridge the digital divide faced by the island of Moloka'i.

Researched economic and infrastructural connectivity barriers affecting development on Moloka’i; engaged with local residents and stakeholders by performing community outreach to identify and work towards bridging the gaps in digital access and economic opportunities.

Co-designed and implemented Moloka’i Connect, a digital resource aggregator cataloging past and present community resources for locals, presented the final solution to the Lieutenant Governor of Hawai’i.
Coach kids of various ages through a sports curriculum, including active involvement in games, promoting teamwork and partnership, enhancing communication among peers, and promoting fun
Streamlined technical support for students and faculty across University of Hawai’i campuses.
Performed culturally choreographed shows for onboard guests
Entertain, teach, and interact with guests in regards to performances
Guide guests through hula lessons and produce welcoming environment
Assisted customers with finding certain items, checking out, and friendly conversation
Worked the floor for security purposes and for customer service needs
Bussed tables, assisted servers, interacted with guests, and carried out lots of other varying responsibilities

Activities.summary: The world's largest educational and scientific computing society
Uh Manoa club that focuses on the development of student produced game projects.
